title: Compounds for use in treating dravet syndrome and other disorders
abstract: The present invention relates to compounds, which are useful for enhancing Na v 1.1 activity, and more particularly for use in the treatment or prevention of a disorder susceptible of being improved by activating the Na v 1.1 channel, such as Dravet syndrome.
claims:
Claims 1. A compound, selected in the group consisting of the peptide JzTx-34, consisting of the amino acid sequence: ACREWLGGCSKDADCCAHLECRKKWPYHCVWDWTV (SEQ ID NO: 1), and containing three disulfide bridges, between cysteines at position 2 and 16, between cysteines at positions 9 and 21, and between cysteines at positions 15 and 29, respectively; and a peptidomimetic thereof, for use in the treatment or prevention of a disorder susceptible of being improved by activating Na v l.l channel. 2. A compound for use according to claim 1, wherein the compound is the peptide JzTx-34. 3. A compound for use according to claim 1, wherein the compound is a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which is derived from the peptide JzTx-34, by one or more of the following modifications: (i) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 comprises or consists essentially of the peptide JzTx-34 ; (ii) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34, by substitution of one or more amino acids in SEQ ID NO: 1, wherein the resulting amino acid sequence has at least 70% sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1 ; (iii) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34, by insertion and/or deletion of one or more amino acids in SEQ ID NO: 1, wherein the amino acids to be deleted do not include cysteines; 

 (iv) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by substitution of one or more L-amino acid(s) by corresponding D-amino acid(s) ; (v) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by replacement of one or more disulfide bond(s) by diselenide, lanthionine, lactam or dimethylene bond(s) ; (vi) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by modifications at the N-terminus ; (vii) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by modifications at the C-terminus ; (viii) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by alkylation of one or more amide bond(s) ; (ix) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by replacement of one or more amide bond(s) CO-NH with another bond selected from the group consisting of: CH=CH, CH2-NH and CH2-SH ; (x) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by connection of the side chain of one or more amino acid(s) to the nitrogen of the peptide backbone, instead of the a-carbon ; (xi) the peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 is derived from the peptide JzTx-34 by replacement of a-carbon of one or more amino acid(s) by nitrogen. 4. A compound for use according to claim 1 or 3, wherein the compound is a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which has at least 70% sequence identity, preferably 80% sequence identity, with SEQ ID NO: 1. 5. A compound for use according to any one of claims 1, 3 or 4, wherein the compound is a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which is derived from the peptide JzTx- 

 34 by substitution of one or more amino acids of SEQ ID NO: 1, selected from the group consisting of: (i) Substitution of two cysteines (C) involved in the same disulfide bridge in the peptide JzTx-34, by selenocysteines (U), in which case the disulfide bridge is replaced by a diselenide bridge (ii) Substitution of an arginine (R) by a lysine (K) (iii) Substitution of a lysine (K) by an arginine (R) (iv) Substitution of a leucine (L) by an isoleucine (I) or a valine (V) (v) Substitution of a valine (V) by a leucine (L) or an isoleucine (I) (vi) Substitution of an aspartic acid (D) by a glutamic acid (E) (vii) Substitution of a glutamic acid (E) by an aspartic acid (D) (viii) Substitution of an alanine (A) by a glycine (G) (ix) Substitution of a glycine (G) by an alanine (A) (x) Substitution of a tryptophan (W) by a tyrosine (Y) (xi) Substitution of a tyrosine (Y) by a tryptophan (W) (xii) Substitution of a threonine (T) by a serine (S) (xiii) Substitution of a serine (S) by a threonine (T) 6. A compound for use according to any one of claims 1, 3 or 4, wherein the compound is a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which is derived from the peptide JzTx- 34 by substitution of one or more amino acids of SEQ ID NO: 1, selected from the group consisting of: Substitution of W5 by Leu, lie, Phe, Tyr, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Asn or Gin, Substitution of L6 by lie, Val, Ala, Gly or Phe, - Substitution of H18 by Arg, Lys, Trp or Tyr, Substitution of L19 by lie, Val, Ala, Gly or Phe, Substitution of R22 by Lys, His or Gin, Substitution of K23 by Arg, His or Asn, Substitution of W25 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, 

 Substitution of P26 by Val, Phe, Leu, lie or Gly, Substitution of H28 by Arg, Lys, Trp, Tyr, Substitution of V30 by lie, Leu, Ala, Gly or Phe, Substitution of W31 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, Substitution of D32 by Glu, Asn or Gin, Substitution of W33 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, Substitution of T34 by Ser or Cys, and/or Substitution of one or more of the remaining amino acids at other positions in SEQ ID NO: 1 by any other amino acid. 7. A compound for use according to any one of claims 1, 3 or 4, wherein the compound is a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which comprises any of the following amino acid sequences : ACRELLGGCSKDSDCCAHLECRKKWPYHCVWDWTI (SEQ ID NO: 2) ACRELLGGCSKDSDCCAHLECRKKWPYHCVWDWTF (SEQ ID NO: 3) CRYLMGGCSKDGDCCEHLVCRTKWPYHCVWDWTF (SEQ ID NO: 4) CTKLLGGCKTDAECCPHLGCRKKWPYHCGWD (SEQ ID NO: 5) CTKLLGGCTKDSECCPHLGCRKKWPYHCGWDGT (SEQ ID NO: 6) 8. A compound for use according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the disorder susceptible of being improved by activating Na v l.l channel is triggered by loss-of- function mutations in the SCN1A gene. 9. A compound for use according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the disorder susceptible of being improved by activating Na v l.l channel is selected from the group consisting of epilepsy (and in particular febrile seizures, generalized epilepsy with febrile seizures plus, Dravet syndrome, borderline severe myoclonic epilepsy of infancy, familial febrile seizures 3A, intractable childhood epilepsy with 

 generalized tonic clonic seizures and temporal epilepsy), Alzheimer's disease and its associated cognitive dysfunction, autism spectrum disorders (and in particular, autism, and more particularly, familial autism), schizophrenia, familial hemiplegic migraines and Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). 10. A compound for use according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the disorder susceptible of being improved by activating Na v l.l channel is Dravet syndrome. 11. A peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34, which is derived from said peptide JzTx- 34, by one or more of the following modifications: (i) substitution, insertion and/or deletion of one or more amino acids in SEQ ID NO: 1, wherein the amino acids to be deleted do not include cysteines, and wherein the resulting amino acid sequence has at least 70% sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1, and does not comprise the amino acid sequence SEQ ID NO: 1, SEQ ID NO: 2, SEQ ID NO: 3, SEQ ID NO: 4, SEQ ID NO: 5 or SEQ ID NO: 6 ; (ii) substitution of one or more L-amino acid(s) by corresponding D-amino acid(s) ; (iii) replacement of one or more disulfide bond(s) by diselenide, lanthionine, lactam or dimethylene bond(s) ; (iv) modifications at the N-terminus ; (v) modifications at the C-terminus ; (vi) alkylation of one or more amide bond(s) ; (vii) replacement of one or more amide bond(s) CO-NH with another bond selected from the group consisting of: CH=CH, CEh-NH and CEh-SH ; (viii) connection of the side chain of one or more amino acid(s) to the nitrogen of the peptide backbone, instead of the a-carbon ; (ix) replacement of a-carbon of one or more amino acid(s) by nitrogen. 12. A peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 according to claim 11, which is derived from said peptide JzTx-34, by substitution of one or more amino acids of SEQ ID NO: 1, selected from the group consisting of: (i) Substitution of two cysteines (C) involved in the same disulfide bridge in the peptide JzTx-34, by selenocysteines (U), in which case the disulfide bridge is replaced by a diselenide bridge (ii) Substitution of an arginine (R) by a lysine (K) (iii) Substitution of a lysine (K) by an arginine (R) (iv) Substitution of a leucine (L) by an isoleucine (I) or a valine (V) (v) Substitution of a valine (V) by a leucine (L) or an isoleucine (I) (vi) Substitution of an aspartic acid (D) by a glutamic acid (E) (vii) Substitution of a glutamic acid (E) by an aspartic acid (D) (viii) Substitution of an alanine (A) by a glycine (G) (ix) Substitution of a glycine (G) by an alanine (A) (x) Substitution of a tryptophan (W) by a tyrosine (Y) (xi) Substitution of a tyrosine (Y) by a tryptophan (W) (xii) Substitution of a threonine (T) by a serine (S) (xiii) Substitution of a serine (S) by a threonine (T) 13. A peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 according to claim 11, which is derived from said peptide JzTx-34, by substitution of one or more amino acids of SEQ ID NO: 1, selected from the group consisting of : Substitution of W5 by Leu, lie, Phe, Tyr, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Asn or Gin, Substitution of L6 by lie, Val, Ala, Gly or Phe, - Substitution of H18 by Arg, Lys, Trp or Tyr, Substitution of L19 by lie, Val, Ala, Gly or Phe, Substitution of R22 by Lys, His or Gin, Substitution of K23 by Arg, His or Asn, Substitution of W25 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, 

 Substitution of P26 by Val, Phe, Leu, lie or Gly, Substitution of H28 by Arg, Lys, T rp, Tyr, Substitution of V30 by lie, Leu, Ala, Gly or Phe, Substitution of W31 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, - Substitution of D32 by Glu, Asn or Gin, Substitution of W33 by Tyr, Phe, Val, Thr, Ser, Met, Leu, lie, Gin or Asn, Substitution of T34 by Ser or Cys, and/or Substitution of one or more of the remaining amino acids at other positions in SEQ ID NO: 1 by any other amino acid ; wherein the resulting amino acid sequence has at least 70% sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1, and does not comprise the amino acid sequence SEQ ID NO: 1, SEQ ID NO: 2, SEQ ID NO: 3, SEQ ID NO: 4, SEQ ID NO: 5 or SEQ ID NO: 6. 14. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a peptidomimetic of the peptide JzTx-34 according to any of claims 11 to 13, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier, vehicle and/or diluent.
